action_readiness.explanation: Readiness increases with up to four distinct response or next-step actions.

clarity_capacity.explanation: Recorded clarity supports prioritization and a bounded next step.

constraint_manageability.explanation: Manageability reflects the recorded controllability of constraints; no listed constraints defaults to full manageability.

energy_capacity.explanation: Recorded energy is treated as available capacity, not motivation or character.

impact_buffer.explanation: Lower recorded impact leaves more near-term recovery room; severity remains visible separately.

pressure_buffer.explanation: Lower recorded pressure increases the room available for deliberate recovery action.

support_capacity.explanation: Recorded access to support increases recovery capacity.

constraint_map.label: Limited review window
constraint_map.notes: The external review window cannot be extended.

support_map.label: Decision log
support_map.notes: Can centralize decisions and unresolved assumptions.

findings.decision_note: Recorded recovery conditions are assessed as fragile recovery conditions. The composite conditions score is 47.8/100 and must be interpreted with the pressure, constraint, support, capacity, and component maps. Protect available capacity, address the highest-friction condition, and update the record at the next checkpoint.

flags.message: Clarify what can pause, wait, or be delegated.
flags.rationale: Pressure is recorded at 8 or above.

flags.message: Route the dependency to an owner or escalation path.
flags.rationale: Dependency friction is recorded at 8 or above.

confidence.rationale: Confidence reflects recorded context completeness and unresolved contradictions, not certainty about future outcomes.

score_display_policy.message: The composite conditions score must be shown with component explanations and condition maps.

interpretation_limits: Describes recorded recovery conditions, not a person's character.
Does not diagnose mental health, predict outcomes, or replace professional support.
Must not be used for employee ranking, automated eligibility, or performance evaluation.
Generated findings require human review when used for consequential decisions.

retrospective.interpretation_limit: The retrospective records supplied observations and uncertainty; it does not establish causation by itself.
redesign: Require one named decision owner before the review window opens.
Record the decision owner before the next review cycle.
repeat: Use a single shared decision log.
uncertainties: Whether all reviewers can use the same feedback channel.
retrospective.what_changed: Feedback arrived through separate channels and the decision owner was not explicit.
retrospective.what_happened: Conflicting review channels changed the deliverable after the checkpoint.
retrospective.what_was_expected: One decision owner would consolidate feedback before review.
what_was_learned: Decision ownership must be visible before review begins.

learning_loop.review_guidance: Patterns remain proposals until a user accepts, rejects, or corrects them.
learning_loop.system_change_guidance: Link any process change to the records and evidence that motivated it, then record an adopt, revise, defer, or retire decision after the pilot.

recommended_actions.rationale: Preserved from the recorded response or next-step plan.
recommended_actions.title: Document unresolved assumptions

recommended_actions.rationale: Preserved from the recorded response or next-step plan.
recommended_actions.title: Schedule a short stakeholder review

recommended_actions.rationale: Preserved from the recorded response or next-step plan.
recommended_actions.title: Clarify the next decision owner

recommended_actions.rationale: Preserved from the recorded response or next-step plan.
recommended_actions.title: Break the work into a 48-hour recovery task

recommended_actions.rationale: Clarity is at or below the midpoint.
recommended_actions.title: Write a one-sentence definition of recovery for this situation.
Reduce the work to one near-term checkpoint instead of a full reset.

blocker_log.blocked_reason: External reviewer availability may constrain the checkpoint.
blocker_log.title: External reviewer availability may constrain the checkpoint.

checkpoint.reassessment_trigger: Reassess at the checkpoint or sooner if the approval dependency changes.
checkpoint.success_signal: One decision owner, one approved scope, and a dated next checkpoint.

escalation_log: Escalate unresolved decision ownership to the program sponsor after 24 hours.

24_hours.expected_effect: A single decision owner is named.
24_hours.reassessment_trigger: Decision owner remains unclear after 24 hours
24_hours.title: Clarify the next decision owner

72_hours.expected_effect: The work is reduced to one reviewable increment.
72_hours.reassessment_trigger: The 48-hour task cannot be completed within available capacity
72_hours.title: Break the work into a 48-hour recovery task

7_days.expected_effect: Stakeholders confirm one scope and next decision.
7_days.reassessment_trigger: Required reviewers are unavailable by the checkpoint
7_days.title: Schedule a short stakeholder review

7_days.expected_effect: Unresolved assumptions are visible before review.
7_days.title: Document unresolved assumptions

plan_rules: At least one action has a named owner.
Blocked and past-target actions are support signals, not performance judgments.
Reassessment creates a new record revision and preserves prior findings.

scope_decision.notes: Limit recovery work to the decision and evidence needed for the next checkpoint.
